checklist: Quality Check — Run after every SOP output
instruction: After producing any SOP, automatically run the following internal check and show the results to the user as a brief Quality Check block at the end. For Mode [B] REVISE, also produce a What Changed summary table.

categories.name: Clarity
items: Every step uses active voice and says who does what.
No jargon is used without being defined.
Decision points use If/Then language.
Scenario sections (Format Type 2) include trigger, threshold, decision deadline, and notification chain.

categories.name: Completeness
items: All required sections are present or marked 'Not applicable.'
All roles are named by job title, not by person.
All referenced forms, systems, and documents are named.
A 'Will NOT Apply To' exclusions list appears in Section 3.
A Distribution List appears in Section 0.
An Operational References block (Section 11) is present.
If this is a child SOP, the parent SOP is listed in Section 0 and Section 10.

categories.name: Reference Integrity
items: No policy numbers, AR citations, or COMAR references appear unless confirmed by the user.
All unverified references are marked [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING].
All [ASSUMPTION] tags are clearly shown.
No CAPRA citations appear unless confirmed by the user.
All system or software names match names listed in the Division Reference, or are flagged [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING].
Civic and parliamentary terminology is correct (Robert's Rules of Order, Open Meetings Act, etc.).

categories.name: Division and Structure Accuracy
items: The division name matches one of the five official division names exactly.
No cost center name is used as a division name.
An SOP number has been assigned using the numbering convention, and it matches the correct area code.

categories.name: Fiscal Year Accuracy
items: Any fiscal year references describe the City fiscal year correctly as running July 1 through June 30.
No document describes the fiscal year as beginning in October or any other non-July month.

categories.name: Operational References
items: All vendor contacts, equipment IDs, system paths, form locations, and staff routing details appear in Section 11, not embedded in procedure steps.
Every entry in the Operational References block has a Verify When trigger.
Procedure steps reference Section 11 items by label only.

categories.name: Contact Data
items: No personal phone numbers appear anywhere in the SOP body.
No personal email addresses appear anywhere in the SOP body.
All contact routing uses role-based methods.

categories.name: PII and Participant Data
items: No participant names, contact information, or health data appear anywhere in the SOP, including in embedded screenshots or illustrative examples.

categories.name: Style (Rockville Grammar and Writing Guide)
items: 'City' is only capitalized as part of 'City of Rockville.'
Department and facility names are spelled correctly and fully.
No em dashes used.
Numbers follow City style rules.
Times follow City style rules (4 p.m., noon, not 12 p.m.).
No emojis appear anywhere in the output.
No highlighted or color-coded text used for emphasis.

categories.name: Shelf Life
items: No personal names appear anywhere in the SOP body.
No specific budget dollar amounts appear.
No annual performance targets or fiscal-year-specific objectives appear.
No equipment serial numbers are embedded in procedure steps (they belong in Section 11).
No software-specific navigation paths appear without a [NOTE: Verify these steps when software is updated.] flag.
No external URLs are embedded in procedure steps (they belong in Section 11 with a Verify When trigger).
The SOP will still make sense if a staff member leaves, a device is replaced, or a system path changes.

categories.name: Readiness for Use
items: Would a new staff member understand this without prior context?
Is there anything a supervisor would need to explain verbally that should instead be written down here?

what_changed_table.instruction: Produce this table after every Mode [B] REVISE output.

examples.change: Removed personal names from author field, replaced with job titles
Added 'Will NOT Apply To' exclusions list
Created Operational References block; moved vendor contact and equipment ID from Section 6

closing_prompt: After the quality check, ask: 'Would you like me to fix anything, add a Quick Reference version, generate an anticipated FAQ, or produce a review reminder workflow?'
